content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

October 12, 1976

Alvin B. Rutner, M.D.,
F.A.C.S.
El Camino Urology Medical
Group, Inc.
205 South Drive
Mountain View, CA 94040

Dear Dr. Rutner:

This letter is in response to your September 9, 1976,
inquiry concerning your campaign reporting obligations
under the Political Reform Act.

You stated that you are an uncontested candidate for
a position on the El Camino Hospital District Board of
Directors, and that you collected and later posted $710.15
in campaign contributions. The Santa Clara Registrar of Voters
office informed us that since there are three openings on the
Board and only three candidates, none of the candidates' names
will appear on the ballot.

Under these circumstances, you are not required to file
campaign statements as long as all contributions were returned
and no expenditures were made in support of your candidacy.

I apologize for the delay in responding to your letter
and hope that this information will be of assistance. If you
have any additional questions, please contact the staff.

Sincerely,

Natalie E. West
Staff Counsel

NEW:av

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or very faintly printed, with only some minimal marks visible at the top that are too unclear to transcribe with certainty. There is insufficient legible content to provide an accurate transcription of this document.

State of California

Memorandum

To : Jeanne Pritchard                                  Date: September 30, 1976

From : Secretary of State
        Janet Sandlin

Subject:

Nan looked at the attached letter today and decided the FPPC should respond rather than
our office.

[This image appears to be a mostly blank page with two small circular marks or holes near the top, and some very faint marks or text that are not clearly legible. The page is predominantly white/blank.]

State of California

Memorandum

To : Janet Sandlin                                Date : September 24, 1976


                    Barbara Campbell/[signature]
From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

Subject: Letter from Alvin B. Rutner

        Somehow this letter found its way to my desk.
I think it should be answered by someone in the Secretary
of State's office telling him what campaign statement
if any, and when, they should file.

        Call Nan if you have any questions.


                                                 FPPC
                                                 [illegible]

I can see this is a mostly blank page with two circular objects (appearing to be mounting holes or rivets) near the top, and what appears to be a small mark or line in the upper right corner. The rest of the page is white/blank space.

Since there is no readable text content in this document image to transcribe, there is nothing to return.

EL CAMINO UROLOGY MEDICAL GROUP, INC.
205 SOUTH DRIVE, MOUNTAIN VIEW, CALIF. 94040
TELEPHONE 961-8954

AND FILED
In the office of the Secretary of State
of the State of California

SEP 19 1970

ALVIN B. RUTNER, M.D., F.A.C.S.
LARRY H. KRETCHMAR, M.D., F.A.C.S.
JOHN P. FLYNN, M.D.

MARCH FONG EU, Secretary of State

Dear Sir

9/4/70

On or about 5/10/70 I signed
a purchase contract and conditions for board of directors
at El Camino Hospital. Others decided it
Radiation room' and an [illegible]
project - No tentative loans signed
upon also filed for candidates .
In total 570.15 was collected on
computer donations prior to the
loaning filing date 5/3/70. Later in
collection was misconducted, all
collected funds were returned to
No thousand it groups claimed
as no computers was necessary .
I'm not sure when a of
the statment were signed LSD
submit it to your per care. The last
Special committee there[illegible] 9/11/70 LSD
was cancelled.

C.B. Rutner MD

I can see this image is mostly blank/white with only a few small marks visible at the top (two circular shapes that appear to be mounting holes or similar markings, and some faint marks in the upper right corner). The rest of the document appears to be empty white space.

There is no legible text content to transcribe from this image.
